meta.title: Caring Community
meta.description: Coordinate time banking, volunteering, organisations, trusted support, and local impact.

caring_community.title: Caring Community
caring_community.subtitle: Bring time-bank exchange, verified volunteering, trusted organisations, groups, events, resources, and impact signals into one coordinated care workflow.
caring_community.welcome_banner: Welcome! Here you can ask for help, offer your time, or simply record a neighbourly kindness. Every contribution — big or small — makes your community stronger.

hero.title: Caring Community
hero.intro: Caring Community is a place for neighbours to help each other with everyday care — a lift to an appointment, a bit of shopping, or some company for an afternoon. Ask for help when you need it, and offer help when you can.
hero.timebank_note: It works alongside the timebank: when you give your time it's recorded as hours you can use later — the same hours, in one shared balance.

new_notice.title: This is brand new.
new_notice.body: Caring Community has only just launched here and we're still building it. Some features are early previews, and it won't run on its own — it depends on neighbours volunteering to give care. Thank you for being one of the first.

how.title: How it works
how.subtitle: Three simple steps.
how.step1_desc: Tell us what you need in your own words. Your request goes to trusted members nearby.
how.step2_desc: Someone in the community offers to help, and you agree a time that suits you both.
how.step3_desc: The time given is logged as hours, so helpers build up hours they can use or pass on later.

recruit.title: We're looking for our first caregivers
recruit.body: Caring Community only works when people step forward to help. We don't have a ready team of caregivers yet — we're building one. If you can spare a little time, you could be one of the first.

caring_community.your_hours_note: These are the same time credits used across the timebank — one shared balance, not a separate caring-community currency.

get_help.title: Get help
get_help.desc: Ask for support and keep track of the people helping you.

give_help.title: Give help
give_help.desc: Offer your time and skills to neighbours who need a hand.

your_hours.title: Your hours
your_hours.desc: See and use the time credits you've earned.

caregiver_tools.title: Caregiver tools
caregiver_tools.desc: Tools for people giving regular care. They become more useful as our caregiver network grows.

safety.title: Safety & updates
safety.desc: Raise a concern, or follow what's happening locally.

actions.request_help_sub: Ask your community for practical support
actions.offer_favour_sub: Record an informal act of kindness — no credits needed
actions.offer_time_sub: List a service or skill you can share

actions.hour_gift_sub: Give your banked hours to family or friends.

actions.projects_sub: Follow staged municipality and neighbourhood updates.

data_export.intro: This archive contains all data we hold about you in this community: your profile, hours given and received, support relationships, transactions, favours, listings, events, and more. The machine-readable JSON download also satisfies your right to data portability, so you can move your data to another service.

developers.meta_description: Explore the Project NEXUS partner API for OAuth2 authentication, scoped endpoints, signed webhooks, and integration access.
developers.hero_subtitle: A curated REST API for banking, payment, and municipal admin partners. OAuth2 client credentials, signed webhooks, and a sandbox you can hit in five minutes.
developers.request_access_body: Partner API access is provisioned by your community administrator. Contact them to get a client_id / client_secret pair.
developers.feature_oauth_body: Server-to-server authentication. Short-lived bearer tokens, scoped per partner.
developers.feature_curated_body: Only what banks and admin systems actually need: users, listings, wallet, aggregates.
developers.feature_webhooks_body: HMAC-SHA256 signatures, automatic retries, delivery health in your dashboard.
developers.feature_rate_limit_body: Configurable per-minute quotas. 429 responses include Retry-After.
developers.auth_intro: Exchange your client credentials for a short-lived bearer token, then attach it to every request as Authorization: Bearer <token>.
developers.auth_step1_body: Your community admin provisions a client_id and client_secret in the admin console. The secret is shown only once at creation time.
developers.auth_step2_body: POST your credentials to /api/partner/v1/oauth/token. The response includes access_token, expires_in (3600s), and granted scope.
developers.auth_step3_body: Attach the token to the Authorization header. Tokens are scoped — the API will return 403 insufficient_scope if you exceed your grant.
developers.endpoints_intro: Hand-picked REST endpoints. All paths are prefixed with /api/partner/v1. All responses are JSON with API-Version: 2.0 header.

endpoint_descriptions.oauth_token: Exchange client credentials for a bearer token (client_credentials grant).
endpoint_descriptions.users_list: List active members. Add scope users.pii to receive email.
endpoint_descriptions.users_detail: Fetch a single member by ID.
endpoint_descriptions.wallet_balance: Read a member's time-credit balance in hours.
endpoint_descriptions.wallet_credit: Credit time hours from a settled bank transfer. Body: user_id, hours, reference, note?
endpoint_descriptions.aggregates_community: Bucketed community totals (no PII). Useful for partner dashboards.
endpoint_descriptions.webhooks_create: Subscribe to events. Body: event_types[], target_url (https://).

developers.webhooks_intro: Subscribe to events with HTTPS callback URLs. Each delivery includes an X-Partner-Signature header — an HMAC-SHA256 of the raw body, keyed by the subscription secret returned at creation time.
developers.webhook_event_wallet_credited: wallet.credited — fires when a partner credits time hours via /wallet/credit
developers.webhook_signing_body: Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the raw request body using your subscription secret. Compare with the X-Partner-Signature header in constant time.
developers.webhook_create_body: POST /api/partner/v1/webhooks/subscriptions with event_types (array) and target_url (https only). The response includes a one-time secret — store it.

regional_analytics.meta_description: Discover privacy-first regional analytics for municipalities and SME partners using anonymised community trends and aggregate reporting.
regional_analytics.partner_dashboard_meta_description: Private regional analytics dashboard for authorised partners.
regional_analytics.hero_title: Understand your region. Without compromising privacy.
regional_analytics.hero_subtitle: A paid analytics product for municipalities and SME partners. Bucketed aggregates, suppressed segments below N=10, never any individual data.
regional_analytics.feature_trends_body: Track engagement, member activity, and volunteer hours over time - no individuals, just regional aggregates.
regional_analytics.feature_demand_supply_body: See where offers and requests cluster by 3-digit postcode and category, with a privacy-bucketed match rate.
regional_analytics.feature_demographics_body: Age and gender distribution, suppressed below N=10 - perfect for grant applications and impact reporting.
regional_analytics.feature_footfall_body: Anonymised page-view trends across public areas of the platform - measure attention, not identity.
regional_analytics.privacy_footer: All metrics are bucketed and anonymised. Segments with N<10 are suppressed. We never expose individual users, names, or addresses to partners.

basic.label: Basic
basic.description: For small municipalities and community partners.

pro.label: Pro
pro.description: For mid-sized municipalities and SME partners with multiple stakeholders.

enterprise.label: Enterprise
enterprise.description: For city governments and federated networks. Custom modules, custom branding, dedicated success manager.

partner_analytics.no_token_body: Please use the secure link supplied by your account manager. The link includes a unique subscription token.
partner_analytics.error_unauthorized: Your subscription token is invalid or expired.
partner_analytics.error_generic: Something went wrong fetching your analytics. Please retry.
partner_analytics.title: Regional analytics
partner_analytics.subtitle: Privacy-bucketed insights for your region. Refreshed daily.
partner_analytics.privacy_footer: All metrics are bucketed and anonymised. Segments with N<10 are suppressed.
partner_analytics.line_chart_aria: Line chart showing partner activity over time
partner_analytics.bar_chart_aria: Bar chart showing partner performance metrics
partner_analytics.pie_chart_aria: Pie chart showing distribution of partner data
partner_analytics.area_chart_aria: Area chart showing partner trends over time

meta.title: Apply for a Pilot Community

provisioning.hero_title: Apply to host a Pilot Community
provisioning.hero_subtitle: Tell us about your region. We review every application and reply by email within 5 working days.
provisioning.intro: Project NEXUS provisions one community per region. Pick a URL slug, choose your languages, and tell us a bit about how you intend to use the platform.

fields.requested_slug_help: Lowercase letters, numbers and hyphens. Example: zurich-care, glarus.

fields.intended_use_placeholder: A short description of your community goals, target audience, and any partner organisations.

slug_status.invalid_format: Use 3–50 lowercase letters, numbers, or hyphens

install.cta_ios_sub: Add NEXUS to your home screen
install.banner_sub: One tap install — works offline, no app store needed.
install.banner_sub_ios: Add to your home screen — works offline, no app store needed.
install.ios_title: Install on your iPhone or iPad
install.ios_intro: iOS doesn’t support automatic install prompts, but you can add NEXUS to your home screen in three taps:
install.ios_step_1_after: Share button at the bottom of Safari.
install.ios_step_3: Tap Add. NEXUS will appear on your home screen as a standalone app.
install.generic_intro: Look for an install option in your browser's menu:
install.generic_step_1: Open your browser menu, usually a three-dot or three-line icon.
install.generic_step_2: Look for Install app, Install this site, or Add to Home screen.
install.generic_step_3: Confirm to add NEXUS to your device.
install.manual_note: If you do not see the option, your browser may have already installed the app or your device may not support it.
install.android_chrome_intro: Add NEXUS to your home screen from the Chrome menu:
install.android_chrome_step_1: Tap the three-dot menu in the top-right of Chrome.
install.android_chrome_step_2: Tap Install app or Add to Home screen.
install.android_chrome_step_3: Confirm. NEXUS will appear on your home screen as a standalone app.
install.samsung_intro: Add NEXUS to your home screen from the Samsung Internet menu:
install.samsung_step_1: Tap the menu icon at the bottom of Samsung Internet.
install.samsung_step_2: Tap Add page to, then Home screen.
install.samsung_step_3: Confirm. NEXUS will appear on your home screen.
install.firefox_android_intro: Add NEXUS to your home screen from the Firefox menu:
install.firefox_android_step_1: Tap the three-dot menu in Firefox.
install.firefox_android_step_2: Tap Install or Add to Home screen.
install.firefox_android_step_3: Confirm to add NEXUS to your home screen.
install.chrome_desktop_intro: Chrome may not have offered the prompt yet. You can still install NEXUS from Chrome's menu:
install.chrome_desktop_step_1: Click the three-dot menu in the top-right of Chrome.
install.chrome_desktop_step_2: Click Cast, save, and share, then Install page as app, or look for an install icon in the address bar.
install.chrome_desktop_step_3: Click Install. NEXUS will open in its own window like a native app.
install.edge_desktop_intro: Install NEXUS as a desktop app from the Edge menu:
install.edge_desktop_step_1: Click the three-dot menu in the top-right of Edge.
install.edge_desktop_step_2: Click Apps, then Install this site as an app.
install.edge_desktop_step_3: Click Install. NEXUS will open in its own window.
install.firefox_desktop_intro: Firefox on desktop does not currently support installing web apps. You can still pin NEXUS for quick access:
install.firefox_desktop_step_1: Bookmark this page with Ctrl+D or pin the tab from the tab context menu.
install.firefox_desktop_step_2: For a true installable app, try Chrome, Edge, or our mobile app.

release_status.summary: Project NEXUS is actively evolving, with feature maturity shown transparently across the platform.

biometric.platform_windows_step1: Click "This PC" to create a passkey stored on this computer. You'll confirm with your Windows Hello PIN, fingerprint, or face.
biometric.platform_windows_step2: Requirement: You must have Windows Hello set up first. Go to Windows Settings > Accounts > Sign-in options > PIN to set it up.
biometric.platform_windows_step3: Or click "Phone, tablet, or security key" to scan a QR code with your phone instead.
biometric.platform_windows_step4: To set up passkeys on your phone too, open this page on your phone and tap "This device".
biometric.platform_mac_step1: Click "This Mac" — your browser will prompt Touch ID or your Mac password.
biometric.platform_mac_step2: The passkey syncs via iCloud Keychain to your iPhone, iPad, and other Macs automatically.
biometric.platform_mac_step3: Or click "Phone, tablet, or security key" to register a different device.
biometric.platform_iphone_step1: Tap "This device" to create a passkey using Face ID or Touch ID.
biometric.platform_iphone_step2: The passkey is saved to iCloud Keychain and works on all your Apple devices.
biometric.platform_iphone_step3: You can also tap "Phone, tablet, or security key" to register a security key.
biometric.platform_ipad_step1: Tap "This device" to create a passkey using Face ID or Touch ID.
biometric.platform_ipad_step2: The passkey is saved to iCloud Keychain and works on all your Apple devices.
biometric.platform_ipad_step3: You can also tap "Phone, tablet, or security key" to register a security key.
biometric.platform_android_step1: Tap "This device" to create a passkey using your fingerprint, face, or screen lock.
biometric.platform_android_step2: The passkey is saved to Google Password Manager and works on all your Android devices and Chrome browsers.
biometric.platform_android_step3: You can also tap "Phone, tablet, or security key" to register a security key.
biometric.platform_linux_step1: Click "This device" — your browser will use its built-in passkey manager.
biometric.platform_linux_step2: Or click "Phone, tablet, or security key" to use a USB security key or scan a QR code with your phone.
biometric.platform_unknown_step1: Click "This device" to create a passkey on the device you're using now.
biometric.platform_unknown_step2: Or click "Phone, tablet, or security key" to register a different device.

enterprise.config_group_general_desc: Site identity, localization, and footer text.
enterprise.config_site_name_desc: Public name shown in the header, browser tab, and emails.
enterprise.config_site_description_desc: Short tagline used in metadata and shared links.
enterprise.config_contact_email_desc: Address members use to reach support.
enterprise.config_contact_phone_desc: Public phone number for support enquiries.
enterprise.config_timezone_desc: IANA timezone identifier used for scheduling and timestamps.
enterprise.config_footer_text_desc: Charity number, company registration, or other legal text displayed in the footer.
enterprise.config_locale_desc: Language used for new members until they pick their own.
enterprise.config_group_registration_desc: Who can sign up, what they verify, and how they are welcomed.
enterprise.config_registration_enabled_desc: Turn off to close sign-ups entirely.
enterprise.config_require_approval_desc: New accounts must be approved by an admin before they can sign in.
enterprise.config_require_email_verification_desc: Members must verify their email address before accessing the platform.
enterprise.config_maintenance_mode_desc: Read-only. Toggle via scripts/maintenance.sh on the server.
enterprise.config_onboarding_enabled_desc: Walk new members through profile and skills setup after sign-up.
enterprise.config_welcome_message_desc: Shown to new members on their first sign-in.
enterprise.config_group_wallet_desc: Time-credit balance, transfer caps, and how the currency is named.
enterprise.config_starting_balance_desc: Time credits granted to new members on sign-up. Set to 0 to disable.
enterprise.config_max_transaction_desc: Largest single transfer between members. Set to 0 for no limit.
enterprise.config_currency_name_desc: Display name for the time-bank currency.
enterprise.config_currency_symbol_desc: Short symbol shown next to balances.
enterprise.config_group_content_desc: Approval rules and content filters for listings and posts.
enterprise.config_auto_approve_listings_desc: Publish new listings immediately instead of holding them for moderator review.
enterprise.config_auto_approve_blog_desc: Publish new blog posts immediately instead of holding them for moderator review.
enterprise.config_max_listing_images_desc: Upper limit on photos attached to a single listing.
enterprise.config_profanity_filter_desc: Block submissions containing words on the platform's profanity list.
enterprise.config_group_notifications_desc: Default delivery channels for new accounts and digest cadence.
enterprise.config_email_notifications_enabled_desc: Default for new accounts. Members can override in their own settings.
enterprise.config_push_notifications_enabled_desc: Default for new accounts. Members can override in their own settings.
enterprise.config_digest_frequency_desc: How often the activity digest email is sent.
enterprise.config_max_listings_per_user_desc: Most listings a single member can have active at once. Set to 0 for no limit.
enterprise.config_max_groups_per_user_desc: Most groups a single member can join. Set to 0 for no limit.
enterprise.config_max_file_upload_mb_desc: Per-file upload size limit. Larger files are rejected at the server.
enterprise.validation_email: Enter a valid email address (e.g., name@example.com)
enterprise.validation_url: Enter a valid URL that starts with http:// or https://
